homepage.text_excerpt: Barry Gardiner Home About Barry Latest News Barry in Parliament Barry in the Commons Barry in Select Committees All Party Parliamentary Groups Westminster Hall Debates Barry in Brent Barry's views on issues of policy Barry on the media What Barry can help with Archive Barry Gardiner Home About Barry Latest News Barry in Parliament Barry in the Commons Barry in Select Committees All Party Parliamentary Groups Westminster Hall Debates Barry in Brent Barry's views on issues of policy Barry on the media What Barry can help with Archive Hello and welcome to my website. My name is Barry Gardiner and I am the Labour MP for Brent West. To get in touch with me please email barry.gardiner.mp@parliament.uk LATEST NEWS Statement: Attack on kenton synagogue The hate-filled attack on the Kenton Synagogue is the latest in a series of disgusting attacks on Jewish buildings. But these are more than attacks on buildings they are calculated attacks on the whole Jewish community, designed to destabilise and instill widespread fear. This is the very definition of terrorism. I am grateful to the police for their swift response and for the arrests they have made in relation to some of the other attacks, but I believe it is vital that the Met police does treat these as terrorist attacks not simply as criminal damage. I also would ask that the Home Affairs Select Committee examines the involvement of foreign actors and the ease with which they are able to launch such attacks. For too long London has been regarded by some hostile regimes as a safe base from which to operate and where money can be laundered. That British Jewry lives under the dark shadow of antisemitic hatred is something which sadly we all have come to understand, but must NEVER come to accept. I pay tribute to the work of the CST and to the security personnel at Kenton who are always there to greet those of us who visit and to ensure the safety of the whole congregation. In the London Borough of Brent we like to pride ourselves on the multicultural nature of our community and the harmony between all those from different faith and cultural backgrounds. But good community relations have to be worked at. They are not built on the simple toleration of the views of others. They are built on respect for our different traditions and the robust challenging of hate speech towards anyone wherever we encounter it. It is up to us all to call out the low level abuse and intolerance, because we all know that these are what facilitate and enable the escalation of a climate of hatred and violence. barry questions the environment secretary on the water industry On Tuesday 10th March, Barry questioned the Environment Secretary, Emma Reynolds, about the government’s proposals for what they call a ‘once-in-a-generation’ reform of the water sector. casework Campaign responses and petitions I receive hundreds of standardised campaign letter emails regarding Government policy and international affairs on just about every domestic and foreign issue. As these emails are sent to me in a template format, please understand that I will only publish a response here as responding individually would take a disproportionate amount of time. Barry's Positions on issues of policy You can find useful and emergency contacts at https://www.barrygardiner.com/what-barry-can-help-with what barry has been up to Recent news need help? Barry has been helping constituents for over 25 years and is here to support and represent constituents of Brent West Find Out How Barry Can Help Learn more Back to Top Privacy Policy Contact details All correspondence via Royal Mail should be addressed to Barry Gardiner MP, House of Commons, London SW1A 0AA or email: barry.gardiner.mp@parliament.uk Promoted by Jackie George on behalf of Barry Strachan Gardiner both at Pavitt Hall, Union Road, Wembley HA0 4AU.

pages.title: About Barry — Barry Gardiner
pages.text_excerpt: About Barry — Barry Gardiner Home About Barry Latest News Barry in Parliament Barry in the Commons Barry in Select Committees All Party Parliamentary Groups Westminster Hall Debates Barry in Brent Barry's views on issues of policy Barry on the media What Barry can help with Archive Barry Gardiner Home About Barry Latest News Barry in Parliament Barry in the Commons Barry in Select Committees All Party Parliamentary Groups Westminster Hall Debates Barry in Brent Barry's views on issues of policy Barry on the media What Barry can help with Archive About Barry Gardiner Barry served as Labour’s Shadow Secretary of State for International Trade and Shadow Minister for International Climate Change from 2016 to 2020 after being first elected to parliament in 1997. Today people in Brent West know Barry as one of our most effective politicians. He gets things done locally and pioneers changes to our laws in the House of Commons. During Barry’s time in office he has significantly improved education, welfare, housing and the local economy in Brent West. Over the last 28 years, Barry has helped the residents of Brent West: Deliver free nursery places for all three and four year olds Build Wembley Primary School, and the Jewish Free School Secure £80million pounds for the complete rebuild of Alperton Community School and Copland School. Rebuild Wembley Park and Wembley Central stations Rejuvenate the economy of Wembley High Road. Taken up over 30,000 cases for constituents. Led the successful campaign to stop fire and rehire Barry in Westminster Barry was Labour’s Shadow Secretary of State for International Trade and Shadow Minister for International Climate Change. He loyally served in this role since 2016 until 2020 . Barry was first appointed to Labour’s shadow cabinet in June 2016, leading on energy and climate change. With changes in government departments after the vote to leave the European Union, he was appointed the Shadow Secretary of State for International Trade, Europe, Energy and Climate Change in July 2016. In government, Barry served as a minister three departments: heading up Education, Employment and Trade in the Northern Ireland Office; responsible for Delivery and Efficiency at Trade & Industry; and as the world’s first Minister for Biodiversity in the Department of Environment, Food & Rural Affairs. He served as the Prime Minister’s Special Envoy on Forestry between 2007 and 2008. Barry has always been very active in parliament, serving on the Environment, Food and Rural Affairs Select Committee, the Energy and Climate Change Select Committee, the Public Accounts Select Committee and on the Broadcasting and Procedure Select Committees. He has stood on the Finance Standing Committee and scrutinised the budget for three years in a row and played a significant role in scrutinising the Energy Bill through his membership of the Energy Bill Committee. Barry also founded and was the long-time Chair of Labour Friends of India for which he was awarded the Padma Shri. Barry has been the International Chair of the Global legislators Organisation (GLOBE) and is a Director of the Climate Parliament. Barry currently serves as a Member of two select committees: The Environmental Audit Select Committee, and The Environment, Food and Rural Affairs Select Committee. He also chairs the All Party Group for Nature and the All Party Group for International Conservation. Back to Top Privacy Policy Contact details All correspondence via Royal Mail should be addressed to Barry Gardiner MP, House of Commons, London SW1A 0AA or email: barry.gardiner.mp@parliament.uk Promoted by Jackie George on behalf of Barry Strachan Gardiner both at Pavitt Hall, Union Road, Wembley HA0 4AU.

pages.title: Latest News — Barry Gardiner
pages.text_excerpt: Latest News — Barry Gardiner Home About Barry Latest News Barry in Parliament Barry in the Commons Barry in Select Committees All Party Parliamentary Groups Westminster Hall Debates Barry in Brent Barry's views on issues of policy Barry on the media What Barry can help with Archive Barry Gardiner Home About Barry Latest News Barry in Parliament Barry in the Commons Barry in Select Committees All Party Parliamentary Groups Westminster Hall Debates Barry in Brent Barry's views on issues of policy Barry on the media What Barry can help with Archive Palestine Action Hunger Strike A group of prisoners being held on remand for accusations relating to Palestine Action have been on hunger strike since the beginning of November. So far, ministers are refusing to meet with their lawyers. Barry has written to David Lammy, the Secretary of State for Justice, to ensure the human rights of his constituents are protected. Marine policy at Labour Party Conference At the Labour Party Conference in Liverpool at the end of September, Barry met with the Blue Marine Foundation to talk about the government’s approach to fishing, and the need to follow the science on quotas. Letter to the PM about Gazan scholarship students In August, Barry was part of a large group of over 100 MPs who signed a letter to the Prime Minister, urging him to facilitate the evacuation of students from Gaza who have earned scholarships to study in the UK. You can read the letter here. Air India Disaster My deepest condolences to the families whose loved ones have been involved in the horrific crash of AI 171. The Indian Government has set up an Operational Control Room at the Ministry of Civil Aviation to coordinate all details. Contact: 011-24610843 | 9650391859 For any of my constituents who have relatives involved in the disaster and who may require Consular Assistance from the British High Commission in India please email me with full details of your relative, including their address and the assistance required. Please put “Air India Disaster” as the subject reference to your email to ensure my office acts as quickly as possible. You can email me on: barry.gardiner.mp@parliament.uk The UK Foreign Office @FCDOGovUK is working with authorities in India to urgently establish the facts of this disaster and to provide support to those involved. Anybody who requires consular assistance or who has concerns about friends and family should call 020 7008 5000. Back to Top Privacy Policy Contact details All correspondence via Royal Mail should be addressed to Barry Gardiner MP, House of Commons, London SW1A 0AA or email: barry.gardiner.mp@parliament.uk Promoted by Jackie George on behalf of Barry Strachan Gardiner both at Pavitt Hall, Union Road, Wembley HA0 4AU.

pages.title: All Party Parliamentary Groups — Barry Gardiner
pages.text_excerpt: All Party Parliamentary Groups — Barry Gardiner Home About Barry Latest News Barry in Parliament Barry in the Commons Barry in Select Committees All Party Parliamentary Groups Westminster Hall Debates Barry in Brent Barry's views on issues of policy Barry on the media What Barry can help with Archive Barry Gardiner Home About Barry Latest News Barry in Parliament Barry in the Commons Barry in Select Committees All Party Parliamentary Groups Westminster Hall Debates Barry in Brent Barry's views on issues of policy Barry on the media What Barry can help with Archive Barry Gardiner October 23, 2025 Barry launches the APPG for National Parks and National Landscapes Barry Gardiner October 23, 2025 On Tuesday 21st October Barry was nominated as Chair of the APPG for National Parks and National Landscapes, a new All Party Parliamentary Group championing access to and protection of National Parks and National Landscapes, which cover 25% of England and Wales. Over 25 MPs and Lords attended the launch, which was co-organised by the Campaign for National Parks, who will be the secretariat for the group, with support from the National Landscapes Association. The APPG will look to help deliver the UK Government’s commitment to update the National Parks and Access to the Countryside Act 1949 - a once-in-a-generation chance to equip protected landscapes with new legislation to deliver more for both nature and people. National Parks and Landscapes are among our country’s greatest assets. They cover 10% of England and 20% of Wales (including 20% of England’s coastline), safeguard rare species and vital carbon stores, and underpin rural economies while supporting the health and wellbeing of millions. But they face mounting pressures: only 6% of National Park land is effectively managed for nature, most rivers are polluted, woodland expansion has stalled, and access remains unequal; just 6% of visitors are under 25, and only 1% from ethnic minority backgrounds. The mission of the APPG will be to drive Government action to protect and revive our National Parks and Landscapes. Barry Gardiner September 9, 2025 Corruption and wildlife crime Barry Gardiner September 9, 2025 On Thursday 4th September, the APPG for International Conservation held a joint session with the APPG on Anti-Corruption and Responsible Tax, chaired by Joe Powell. The joint session heard evidence from four speakers about the links between corruption and wildlife crime: Ben Brock, Senior Researcher at TRAFFIC, explained the intersection of corruption and environmental crime specific to the illegal wildlife trade. Jo Shaw, Executive Director of Save the Rhino, presented a case study on the role of corruption in rhino horn crimes around Kruger National Park in South Africa Tom Johnson, Director of the Gecko Project, provided a case study on how jurisdictions facilitate the destruction of Indonesia’s rainforests. Lisa Hartevelt, Chair of the Environmental Crime and Corruption Working Group at the UNCAC Coalition, discussed the efforts to raise environmental crime at the upcoming UNCAC CoSP in Abu Dhabi. (back row) Lord Randall, Barry, and Joe Powell MP. (front row) Tom Johnson, Lisa Hartevelt, Jo Shaw, and Ben Brock Barry Gardiner June 24, 2025 Barry launches the APPG on Bees, Pollinators, and Invertebrates Barry Gardiner June 24, 2025 Barry launched the All Party Parliamentary Group on Bees, Pollinators, and Invertebrates. The Royal Entomological Society (RES), the UK’s leading voice on insect science, will serve as the group’s secretariat, with Barry acting as Chair. At the launch, Barry said: “Insects are a foundational part of life on earth – they pollinate our crops, enrich our soils and support our ecosystems but we are experiencing a climate crisis which is resulting in global biodiversity loss. As parliamentarians, we have a unique opportunity to better understand the role insects play in our environment and to encourage measures that support their recovery. I’m lookin

pages.title: What Barry can help with — Barry Gardiner
pages.text_excerpt: What Barry can help with — Barry Gardiner Home About Barry Latest News Barry in Parliament Barry in the Commons Barry in Select Committees All Party Parliamentary Groups Westminster Hall Debates Barry in Brent Barry's views on issues of policy Barry on the media What Barry can help with Archive Barry Gardiner Home About Barry Latest News Barry in Parliament Barry in the Commons Barry in Select Committees All Party Parliamentary Groups Westminster Hall Debates Barry in Brent Barry's views on issues of policy Barry on the media What Barry can help with Archive Surgery Information: Barry hosts regular surgeries at Brent Civic Centre, Engineers Way, Wembley HA9 0FJ . To request a surgery appointment please contact Barry with details of your case and any copies of relevant correspondence to either barry.gardiner.mp@parliament.uk or via post to Barry Gardiner MP House of Commons SW1A 0AA Help & Advice: Who can Barry help? Barry can only help in cases raised by people who live in his constituency, which covers the wards of Alperton, Barnhill, Fryent, Kenton, Northwick Park, Preston, Queensbury, Sudbury, and Wembley Central. If you are not a constituent please contact your local MP. You can find out who your local MP is by entering your postcode here . Please note that if you are contacting Barry on behalf of a friend or family member he will need their signed and written consent before he is able to take action. Barry can help you with any matter for which Parliament or central government is responsible, such as matters relating to : - Benefits, pensions or allowances – please have your National Insurance Number ready when contacting the office. - Immigration and Asylum - please have your Full Name/Date of birth/Country of Origin/Home Office reference number ready when contacting the office. - Queries and complaints about the NHS - Issues regarding tax - Education Barry doesn't have any control over Brent Council and if your problem relates to the local authority, the first contact should be with your local councillors. If you are not satisfied with their response Barry may be able to assist. Please provide proof that you've approached the council before contacting our office. You can check who your ward councillors are here Brent Council has responsibility for matters such as: Housing – allocations, repairs, problems with landlords Finance – Council tax, Housing and Council Tax Benefit, rent arrears Schools – allocation of school places, complaints Social services – carers, Child Protection Environmental health - noise nuisance, trading standards, public health Planning Waste Services - refuse collection/ street cleaning/fly-tipping Public highways – potholes, road repairs, street lighting, traffic problems Leisure facilities – libraries, parks, sports centres How can Barry help? Barry and his team can help constituents by raising concerns with the relevant department or official or writing to the relevant Minister, many problems can be solved in this way. Our constituent cases are dealt with in terms of how urgent the case is, so please note that due to the volume of correspondence Barry receives, helping constituents with urgent personal problems has to take priority over responding to correspondence on political issues. Some Useful Numbers Emergencies Police, fire, first aid emergencies 999 To report a crime (not in an emergency) 101 Gas Emergencies 080 0111 9999 NHS 111 111 Health Care Northwick Park Hospital 020 8864 3232 Brent Clinical Commissioning Group 020 8900 5300 Transport TfL (Tube and buses) 034 3222 1234 National Rail Enquiries 034 5748 4950 Utility providers UK Power Networks (emergencies from mobiles) 080 0316 3105 UK Power Networks (Customer Services) 080 0028 4587 Thames Water (Blocked Drains) 080 0316 9800 Affinity Water (emergencies) 0345 357 2407 Advice National Debtline 080 8808 4000 24 Hour National Domestic Violence Helpline 080 8200 0247 Asian Women’s Resource Centre 020 8961 5701 Solace Women’s Aid 0
